title: React SPA Architecture
tldr: React SPAs load a single HTML page and dynamically update content via JavaScript, avoiding full page reloads.
The architecture uses a component tree, virtual DOM, and a unidirectional data flow (parent to child via props).
Client-side routing (e.g., React Router) manages URL changes and renders the correct component tree without server round-trips.
State management solutions (Context API, Redux) handle cross-component data sharing across the SPA.
laymanDefinition: Think of a React SPA like a multi-room museum inside a single building. When you enter, you get a map (the initial HTML). Instead of leaving the building to see each new exhibit, you just walk to a different room.

deepDive.heading: Single HTML Shell
deepDive.text: The server sends a minimal HTML file with a single root <div id="root"></div>. React renders the entire UI into this div.

deepDive.heading: Component Tree Architecture
deepDive.text: React applications are structured as a tree of components. Data flows down via props, and actions flow up via callbacks.

deepDive.heading: Client-Side Routing
deepDive.text: React Router enables navigation without page reloads using the History API.

deepDive.heading: State Management Layer
deepDive.text: For complex SPAs with global state, libraries like Redux or Zustand provide a centralized store.

deepDive.heading: Build & Deployment Considerations
deepDive.text: Critical: configure the server to serve index.html for all routes (fallback) so deep links work on refresh.

interviewAnswer: React SPA architecture centers on a single HTML host page where React renders a component tree into a root DOM node. Client-side routing (React Router) intercepts URL changes and swaps components without full page reloads.

interviewQuestions.question: What is a Single Page Application (SPA)?
interviewQuestions.answer: An SPA loads a single HTML page and dynamically updates content via JavaScript. React implements this by rendering a component tree into a root DOM element.

interviewQuestions.question: How does client-side routing work?
interviewQuestions.answer: React Router uses the History API (pushState, replaceState) and a popstate event listener to update the URL and render matching components.

interviewQuestions.question: Advantages and disadvantages of SPA?
interviewQuestions.answer: Advantages: faster navigation, smoother UX, reduced server load. Disadvantages: larger initial bundle, SEO challenges, JavaScript dependency.

interviewQuestions.question: Explain unidirectional data flow.
interviewQuestions.answer: Data flows from parent to child through props. Children use callback functions to communicate upward.

interviewQuestions.question: How do you handle deep linking?
interviewQuestions.answer: The server must serve index.html for all routes (catch-all fallback). React Router renders the correct component on initial load.

interviewQuestions.question: What is code splitting?
interviewQuestions.answer: Dividing the bundle into smaller chunks loaded on demand. React.lazy() and Suspense enable component-level splitting.

interviewQuestions.question: Compare SSR vs CSR.
interviewQuestions.answer: CSR renders in browser after JS loads. SSR renders HTML on server, sending a fully populated page for better SEO.

interviewQuestions.question: How to manage auth state across a SPA?
interviewQuestions.answer: Store tokens in localStorage or cookies, sync with global state on app init. Use protected route wrappers.

interviewQuestions.question: Role of a bundler in a React SPA?
interviewQuestions.answer: Bundles source code (JSX, ES modules, CSS) into optimized static files handling transpilation, code splitting, and tree shaking.

interviewQuestions.question: Explain the 'root' div concept.
interviewQuestions.answer: The HTML shell has <div id="root">. ReactDOM.createRoot().render() attaches the component tree to this element.

codeExamples.title: Basic SPA Structure with Router
codeExamples.description: BrowserRouter wraps the app. Routes define which component renders for each path.

codeExamples.title: API Fetching with Loading State
codeExamples.description: Fetch data on mount, show loading state, then render.

codeExamples.title: Protected Route
codeExamples.description: Checks auth state and redirects if not logged in.

mcqQuestions.question: What is a Single Page Application?
options: Single HTML page updates dynamically via JS
mcqQuestions.explanation: An SPA loads one HTML page and dynamically rewrites content.

mcqQuestions.question: How does client-side routing work?
mcqQuestions.explanation: React Router uses the History API.

mcqQuestions.question: What server config is needed for deep linking?
options: Each route needs its own HTML
mcqQuestions.explanation: Without fallback, server returns 404 for non-root paths.

mcqQuestions.question: What is a disadvantage of SPAs?
mcqQuestions.explanation: All app code must download before first render.

mcqQuestions.question: Where to store auth tokens for persistence?
mcqQuestions.explanation: State is lost on refresh; persist in localStorage.
